title: The Diagonal Wire Cut at Delta-7
description: At splice point Delta-7 in the crater field, the forward copper telephone line has been cut with a single clean stroke at a 45-degree angle. The cut surfaces are bright copper — unoxidized, made within the last two hours. A strand of khaki officer's uniform wool is caught on the wire's severed end. Most conclusively: lying in the mud eighteen inches from the cut, half-concealed under a clod of clay, is a pair of wire cutters with German manufacturer's markings — Feldzange, stamped with the Prussian eagle — that have no business being in an American signals kit.
